channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Ohtani homers in playoff debut and the Dodgers rally to beat the Padres 7-5 in NLDS opener
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani hit a three-run homer in his postseason debut, Teoscar Hernández’s two-run single gave Los Angeles its first lead in a playoff game in two years, and the Dodgers beat the San Diego Padres 7-5 in their NL Division Series opener Saturday.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Padres fandom in full force at first of three postseason watch parties
item.description: The Padres' quest for postseason glory continues and fans are along for the thrilling journey. Despite Saturday night's game being held in Los Angeles at Dodgers Stadium, the first of three watch parties kicked off with a packed house at Petco Park.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Yankees, Royals make MLB history in wild first game of ALDS
item.description: The New York Yankees survived a wild game against the Kansas City Royals on Saturday to take Game 1 of the American League Division Series.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington pulls away in 4th quarter to beat No. 10 Michigan 27-17 in title game rematch
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Washington quarterback Will Rogers watched the final seconds tick away and wondered if the field at Husky Stadium was about to be flooded with purple.
item.dc:creator: TIM BOOTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Verdugo's go-ahead single and acrobatic catch lift Yankees over Royals 6-5 in ALDS opener
item.description: Alex Verdugo hit a tiebreaking single in the seventh inning and saved at least one run with a sliding catch along the left-field line, boosting the New York Yankees over the Kansas City Royals 6-5 in their AL Division Series opener.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mets starter Kodai Senga goes 2 innings and passes baton to brilliant bullpen in Game 1 NLDS win
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Kodai Senga slapped the dugout railing in a raw display of emotion, a most unexpected Game 1 starter pumping his fist during an eighth-inning comeback that — have you followed these New York Mets? — should really have been expected by now.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: ESPN analyst wants anonymous voter who chose Angel Reese for Rookie of the Year to go public: 'Stand on it'
item.description: Only one voter decided Caitlin Clark was not the best rookie in the WNBA this season. An ESPN analyst wants that lone voter to be revealed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: MacDonald claims first varsity win in program history with home triumph over Mills
item.description: MacDonald High School football earned its first-ever varsity win on Friday, a 41-34 victory over Mills in North San Jose. The Condors are playing this season without any seniors as they start their varsity program from scratch.
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Clayton Kershaw won't pitch for the Dodgers in playoffs after big toe injury fails to heal
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Clayton Kershaw, sidelined since late August with a toe injury, won’t pitch again this season for the Los Angeles Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Magic Johnson: Black men should vote for Harris because Trump 'did not deliver on' promises to Black community
item.description: During Kamala Harris' rally in Michigan Saturday, NBA legend Magic Johnson implored Black men to vote for Harris since Donald Trump "did not deliver on" promises to the Black community.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rested Guardians blank Tigers to win ALDS Game 1
item.description: The Cleveland Guardians won Game 1 of their ALDS matchup over the Detroit Tigers behind an explosive five-run first inning. Lane Thomas had a three-run home run in the win.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greg Landry, Lions great and former NFL coach, dead at 77
item.description: Former Detroit Lions quarterback Greg Landry, who later returned as an assistant coach, has died at the age of 77, the team announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Real Salt Lake ready for road test: Unbeaten streak at stake against San Jose Earthquakes
item.description: Real Salt Lake (4th place Western Conference, 53 points) kept a clean sheet at home against Minnesota on Wednesday and now head back on the road with a four-match unbeaten streak to face the San Jose Earthquakes (14th place, Western Conference, 21 points) on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Lane Thomas hits 3-run homer, Cleveland's bullpen torments Tigers as Guardians win ALDS opener 7-0
item.description: Lane Thomas hit a three-run homer in a five-run outburst before Detroit got an out, and the Cleveland Guardians unleashed their lights-out bullpen to complete a four-hitter in a 7-0 win over the Tigers in an AL Division Series opener.
item.dc:creator: Tom Withers, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cal student makes field goal on 'College GameDay,' to fund $600k donation towards Hurricane Helene relief
item.description: A college student made the most of a second chance field goal attempt, by nailing a 33-yard kick to win a $100,000 prize and send $600,000 to Helene relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: High school football player spanks opponents with belt after 77-0 beatdown
item.description: A high school football player in the Houston area spanked his opponents with a belt after beating them 77-0, as part of a new social media trend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gauff rallies to reach China Open final and Sinner, Alcaraz and Djokovic win in Shanghai
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Coco Gauff rallied from a set down for the third consecutive match as she beat Paula Badosa 4-6, 6-4, 6-2 to reach the China Open final on Saturday. The sixth-ranked Gauff will play Karolina Muchova of the Czech Republic in Sunday's final.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Odell Beckham Jr. will make his Dolphins debut vs. Patriots after being activated from PUP
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — The Miami Dolphins activated Odell Beckham Jr. from the physically unable to perform list on Saturday, clearing the way for the veteran receiver to make his season debut Sunday at New England.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LeBron James reveals why he joined the Lakers
item.description: The NBA season is right around the corner, but the league's oldest player still had some time on his hands on Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Cal fans break through ESPN's 'College GameDay' barricades ahead of Miami showdown
item.description: The anticipation of Cal's first ACC home game and the arrival of ESPN’s "College GameDay" crew was too much for some fans to handle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Padres restricting Dodgers fans from buying NLDS tickets in San Diego
item.description: There's a controversy brewing over tickets for the 2024 MLB playoffs. The San Diego Padres are restricting Los Angeles Dodgers fans from buying NLDS tickets for the games in San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, KTLA 5

item.title: George Brett reminds Royals players of intensity of past playoffs against Yankees
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — George Brett watched the Kansas City Royals prepare to face the New York Yankees and remembered the combustible clashes of the 1970s. “This isn’t a series, this is war,” said the Hall of Famer, tossing in a profanity for emphasis.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: La Salle women's field hockey teams calls for end to 'senseless gun violence' after shooting ends game early
item.description: The La Salle field hockey team called for an end to "senseless gun violence" after one of their games ended abruptly due to a nearby shooting last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-MLB star laments limitations on starting pitching, involvement of 'stat nerds' in today's baseball
item.description: Six-time All-Star Will Clark laments his frustrations with starting pitching, 'stat nerds' involvement in today's baseball during an appearance on OutKick's "The Ricky Cobb Show.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ludmila scores in third straight game as Chicago Red Stars down Houston Dash 2-0
item.description: Ludmila scored a goal and earned a penalty shot as the Chicago Red Stars beat the Houston Dash 2-0 on Friday night at Shell Energy Stadium. Ludmila headed in Mallory Pugh's free kick in the 8th minute to get things started. It was the third straight game she had scored a goal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Napheesa Collier scores 26 to help Lynx top Sun 90-81 and take 2-1 lead in semifinal series
item.description: UNCASVILLE, Conn. (AP) — After struggling in the last game offensively, Napheesa Collier wasn't going to let it happen again. The Lynx's star scored 26 points and Minnesota beat the Connecticut Sun 90-81 on Friday night to take a 2-1 advantage in the best-of-five semifinal matchup.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aces avoid elimination against Liberty while Lynx take 2-1 lead in series with Sun
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A return home helped Las Vegas stave off elimination by New York and look like the team that has won consecutive WNBA championships, while Connecticut couldn't stop Minnesota and take advantage of their homecourt.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON and DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fans set to camp out for Cal Bears big game
item.description: (KRON) – It will be a busy weekend for the City of Berkeley as the Cal Bears football team is hosting a college game day show for the first time in over 30 years. The Bears are currently (3-1) and are looking to knock off the eighth-seed and undefeated Miami Hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Is the Chicago Bears run game back?
item.description: The Chicago Bears are relying on their running game and short passing game to replicate their Week 4 performance against the Carolina Panthers, as they have shifted away from a run-dominant offense under their new offensive coordinator.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Yankees playoff tickets cost more in Kansas City than the Bronx
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) --- So, not everything is more expensive in New York City. Yankees fans will shell out less cash for a seat at Yankees Stadium than Kauffman Stadium in Kansas City when the American League Division Series kicks off Saturday at Yankee Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Mira Wassef, PIX 11

item.title: Bucs remain banged up but carry confidence into Thursday's Falcons clash
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers are riding momentum into Thursday night’s NFC South clash with the Atlanta Falcons but so far, the short game week has presented many challenges.
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: High School Boys Golf: Grand Junction shines in regionals, state tournaments set
item.description: This week, the 4A Region 4 tournament took place at Black Canyon Golf Course in Montrose. If we learned anything, it's that the Grand Junction Tigers are for real.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: RSL looks to solidify playoff seeding against Minnesota
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Real Salt Lake has already clinched an MLS playoff spot, but considering RSL is 6-1-2 all-time at home in the postseason, they want that home field advantage in the first round.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the Padres Wild Card Series at Petco Park
item.description: If you are heading to the game, here are some things to know about the best-of-three National League Wild Card Series matchup between the Padres and Atlanta Braves:
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Column: Lightning and thunder combo could bode well for Bears' offense
item.description: The Chicago Bears have developed a formidable 1-2 punch in running backs D'Andre Swift and Roschon Johnson, who have shown the ability to both smash and slice through defenses, but the success of the offense depends on the performance of the offensive line.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Dikembe Mutombo, a Hall of Fame player and tireless advocate, dies at 58 from brain cancer
item.description: Dikembe Mutombo, a Basketball Hall of Famer who was one of the best defensive players in NBA history and a longtime global ambassador for the game, died Monday from brain cancer, the league announced. He was 58.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, WGN 9
